symbols: vertical yellow figure against dark field — a body suspended or offered in sacrifice
cross-like structure — the instrument of sacrifice, the intersection of suffering and transcendence
red accents at the base — blood, the cost of sacrifice
overwhelming darkness — the cosmic weight of suffering and offering
description: An abstract expressionist painting dominated by very dark blues, greens, and blacks. A yellowish-green vertical figure or form is barely discernible at center, suggesting a human body suspended or standing against a dark cross-like structure of green and dark lines. Small accents of red appear near the bottom. The paint is applied thickly with visible texture. The overall composition is vertical and oppressively dark, with the faint figure emerging from the blackness like a presence barely visible through night.
theological_description: This abstract work titled 'The Sacrifice' evokes the primal biblical theme of offering — from Abel's firstfruits (Genesis 4:4) through Abraham's binding of Isaac (Genesis 22:1-14) to the ultimate sacrifice of Christ on the cross. The barely visible figure suspended in darkness recalls the crucifixion during which 'darkness came over the whole land' (Mark 15:33). The abstraction strips sacrifice to its essence: a life offered, a body broken, blood shed in darkness. The theological heart of sacrifice is substitution — 'God made him who had no sin to be sin for us, so that in him we might become the righteousness of God' (2 Corinthians 5:21). The overwhelming darkness may also evoke the anguished cry from the cross: 'My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?' (Matthew 27:46) — the moment when the Son experienced the full weight of separation from the Father.

scripture_refs.reason: Darkness covering the land during the crucifixion and Christ's cry of dereliction
God making Christ to be sin for us — the theological core of sacrifice
The sacrifice of Isaac — the archetypal sacrifice that foreshadows Calvary
